etymology.description: 人(人) (rén, "person") over an arrow tied with 糸(糸) (mì, "silk thread") -> a person hanging a thread
etymology.interpretation: Originally depicting a man above an arrow tied to a silk thread, 吊 captures the image of a person utilizing a thread for attachment or suspension. The semantic component of a thread conveys the action, while the depiction of a man emphasizes direct involvement, leading to the sense of 'hanging'.

examples.chinese: 他把画吊在墙上(他把畫吊在牆上)。
examples.pinyin: Tā bǎ huà diào zài qiáng shàng.
examples.english: He hung the painting on the wall.

examples.chinese: 风筝在空中自由地吊着(風箏在空中自由地吊著)。
examples.pinyin: Fēngzhēng zài kōngzhōng zìyóu de diào zhe.
examples.english: The kite is freely hanging in the air.

examples.chinese: 他用吊车吊起重物(他用吊車吊起重物)。
examples.pinyin: Tā yòng diàochē diào qǐ zhòngwù.
examples.english: He used the crane to lift the heavy object.
